summary: Turn 3 persuasion success revealed filing timestamp 0217 and Harrow Spur Commercial Registry jurisdiction, but Turn 4 Who Filed the Hold thread still says filing timestamp and reference jurisdiction remain obscured.
Turn 5 reads the Sable Margin message and extracts timestamp/exact hold amount, but The Broker's Sealed Offer thread still retains the old clue "message is unread".
Case board contains multiple variants of the hold filing fact; some say filing timestamp/details remain unread or obscured while newer clues establish 0217 and Harrow Spur Commercial Registry.

Moving to the cargo corridor created/displayed inconsistent location names: heading "HARROW SPUR DOCKING ARM4 CORRIDOR", location list "harrow spur docking arm4 corridor" and "Harrow Spur — Docking Arm 4, Cargo-Side Corridor".

Perception success establishes sealed cargo was placed in the corridor tonight via side-access bypass with sealant matching a pipe joint, but visible case board remains limited to hold/broker threads and does not show a cargo/hatch clue.

Turn 12 reveals/accepts the data-core job, Veyne wants it back, and dead-drop destination, but new "Deliver the Data Core to Dead-Drop" thread appears with no attached clues while prior broker thread is removed/decremented.

After Lira explains the release chip but before the player returns to Coll or processes it, the visible "Clear the Forty-Thousand Hold" spine disappears/decrements heavily, making the hold look resolved before the fiction actually clears it.
Perception success reveals Coll notified Sev Anrath / Veyne Logistics and departure monitor is active, but new "Evade Veyne Logistics Tracking" thread appears with no attached clues.
